name: Yappi (Yet Another Python Profiler with Thread Support)

agent_readiness.notes: CPU/Wall time profiler with thread and async (asyncio/gevent) support. import yappi. yappi.start(). do_work(). yappi.stop(). stats = yappi.get_func_stats(). stats.print_all(). stats.save('/tmp/profile.pstat', type='pstat'). yappi.get_thread_stats(). Clock types: yappi.set_clock_type('cpu') or 'wall'. Context types: yappi.set_context_type('asyncio'). profile_thread(): profile specific thread. Sorting: stats.sort('tsub', 'desc'). SnakeViz compatible (save as pstat). Used for profiling multithreaded and async Python. gevent support. Unlike cProfile, yappi accurately profiles multi-threaded code. Context manager: with yappi.run(): do_work(). Differential stats between two runs.

gotchas: No type annotations — limited IDE support
Less mainstream than cProfile + SnakeViz or pyinstrument
Thread-safety of stats collection requires careful handling
asyncio context type must be set before starting profiling
Learning curve for interpreting thread stats
best_when: Profiling multi-threaded Python where cProfile misses thread time
Async Python (asyncio/gevent) profiling with accurate timing
Wall vs CPU time comparison for IO-bound code
When per-thread CPU usage breakdown is needed
avoid_when: Simple single-threaded profiling (pyinstrument or cProfile + SnakeViz is simpler)
summary: Yappi is a CPU/wall-time Python profiler with accurate multi-thread and async support. yappi.start(); do_work(); yappi.stop(); yappi.get_func_stats().print_all(). set_clock_type('cpu'|'wall'). set_context_type('asyncio'). Per-thread stats. SnakeViz-compatible pstat output. MIT. Unlike cProfile, accurate in multi-threaded code. Best for profiling threaded or async Python. Pyinstrument is simpler for single-threaded; py-spy for production.
